content.problemStatement:
Chef has NNN boxes, numbered from 111 to NNN. The ithi^{th}ith box contains AiA_iAi​ candies, which are all of the same color. Also, candies from two different boxes never have the same color.

Your goal is to create as many Groups\text{Groups}Groups as possible, where


A Group\text{Group}Group is a collection of exactly KKK candies.
No two candies in the same Group\text{Group}Group can have the same color.
A particular candy can be included in at most one Group\text{Group}Group. In particular, it is possible that some candy is not part of any Group\text{Group}Group.
Find the maximum number of Groups\text{Groups}Groups you can create using the candies in the NNN boxes.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input:

The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers, NNN and KKK, the number of boxes and the number of candies in each Group\text{Group}Group.
The second line contains NNN space-separated numbers - A1,A2,…,ANA_{1}, A_{2},\ldots ,A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​, the number of candies in the NNN boxes.




Output Format
For each test case, output in a new line, the maximum number of Groups\text{Groups}Groups that can be formed using the candies in the NNN boxes, such that each Group\text{Group}Group consists of exactly KKK candies and no two candies in the same Group\text{Group}Group have the same color.
constraints: 1 ≤ N ≤ 2·10^5 (sum of N over all test cases ≤ 2·10^5)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two lines. The first line contains two integers N and K – the number of boxes and the size of each group. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN, where Ai is the number of candies in the i‑th box (all candies in one box have the same unique colour).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the maximum possible number of groups that can be formed.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: K=1, each candy can form a group alone → 3 groups.
Test case 2: total candies =6 < K=10 → 0 groups.
Test case 3: N=2, K=2, boxes have 5 and 6 candies. Each group needs one candy from each box, so we can form min(5,6)=5 groups.
Test case 4: N=5, K=3, boxes = [6,9,4,2,1]. The maximum number of groups is 6 (verified by binary search).
First test: only one colour, K=1, we can make 100 groups. Second test: K=5 > N=3, impossible to form any group.
Total candies =12, K=3, and N≥K, so we can form 4 groups, each using three different colours.

solution.approach: For a fixed number of groups G we can use at most one candy from each box in every group, therefore box i can contribute at most min(Ai, G) candies overall. The total number of candies that can be placed into G groups is S(G)=∑ min(Ai, G). G groups are feasible iff S(G) ≥ G·K. The predicate is monotone (if G works, any smaller value also works), so we binary‑search the largest G. Upper bound for the search is totalCandies / K. Edge case: if K > N no group can be formed because we need K distinct colours. The algorithm runs in O(N·log(maxPossibleGroups)) per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N·log(∑Ai / K)) per test case, overall O((∑N)·log(10^14)) which fits the limits.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra besides the input array.

code.Python3:
import sys

def max_groups(N, K, A):
    if K > N:
        return 0
    total = sum(A)
    low, high, ans = 0, total // K, 0
    while low <= high:
        mid = (low + high) // 2
        need = mid * K
        s = 0
        for x in A:
            s += x if x < mid else mid
            if s >= need:
                break
        if s >= need:
            ans = mid
            low = mid + 1
        else:
            high = mid - 1
    return ans

it = iter(sys.stdin.read().strip().split())
T = int(next(it))
out = []
for _ in range(T):
    N = int(next(it)); K = int(next(it))
    A = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
    out.append(str(max_groups(N, K, A)))
print('\n'.join(out))
